title: Transactional Outbox, Relay, Inbox, Sagas, and Compensation
description: An HTTP transaction commits database state then publishes, or publishes then rolls back; a relay deletes outbox rows before confirms; a multi-service workflow treats retries as rollback. Learners cumulatively produce outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write.
Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically.
Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity.
